intent: Audit the exact outcome-vector/locus frame under unchanged V8, with one active payoff witness and no capacity, optimization, welfare, novelty, or human-gate claim.

entity.title: Framing audit: rejected-score disclosure and appeal selection
entity.summary: A positive economics audit of the outcome-locus framing, with one active appeal-margin witness and no welfare, capacity, optimization, or human-G1 claim.

tension.conventional_prediction: Because the initial rule, review technology, and final rule are fixed, revealing which rejected score occurred may appear irrelevant to the institution's post-rejection classification outcomes.
tension.countervailing_logic: The same final rule is applied to a different selected appeal pool: score disclosure changes the applicant's posterior before the costly appeal choice and can move a cell across the appeal threshold.
tension.economic_puzzle: When can a pure refinement of rejected applicants' information change who seeks reconsideration, and hence the classification-processing outcome vector, even though every institutional rule is unchanged?
tension.resolution_target: Characterize the open cost and signal regions in which disclosed score cells and the pooled rejection cell lie on opposite sides of the appeal threshold, then decompose their exact outcome-vector contributions.

forces.label: Posterior refinement changes appeal selection
forces.operative_margin: The appeal-versus-no-appeal payoff difference v*A_psi(p)-c at a rejected score-private-signal cell.
forces.economic_logic: Disclosure replaces a pooled posterior with score-specific posteriors. Strict review monotonicity makes anticipated acceptance strictly increasing in that posterior, so an intermediate appeal cost can make some cells enter or exit appeal.
forces.active_when: A positive-probability disclosed cell and the corresponding pooled cell have different A_psi values and c/v lies strictly between them.

causal_chain.cause: Score disclosure refines the pooled low-or-middle rejection message.
causal_chain.endogenous_response: For a fixed private signal, the applicant uses a score-specific rather than pooled qualification posterior.
causal_chain.consequence: The anticipated fixed-rule acceptance probability A_psi(p) differs across the disclosed and pooled information cells.

causal_chain.cause: The posterior changes the continuation-inclusive appeal payoff v*A_psi(p)-c.
causal_chain.endogenous_response: The rejected applicant chooses appeal rather than non-appeal when that payoff difference crosses zero.
causal_chain.consequence: A positive-probability score-private-signal cell enters or exits the selected appeal pool.

active_margin_witness.concrete_state: A rejected applicant has middle score and private signal x under score_disclosure, before the fresh review draw, with posterior p=0.8. The illustrative common primitives are rho_0=0.2, rho_1=0.8, psi(0)=0, psi(1)=1, v=1, and c=0.56.
active_margin_witness.alternative_action: Do not appeal and remain rejected
active_margin_witness.focal_payoff: v*A_psi(0.8)-c = 1*(0.2+0.6*0.8)-0.56 = 0.12, including the fixed continuation review and final decision.
active_margin_witness.alternative_payoff: 0, because a non-appellant remains finally rejected and pays no appeal cost.
active_margin_witness.feasibility_basis: At this pre-review decision node the feasible set is binary: appeal or do not appeal. These two actions exhaust the maintained action set, and both face the same information, beliefs, timing, and continuation rule.
active_margin_witness.best_response_inequality: Appeal is optimal iff v*A_psi(p)-c >= 0. Here 0.12>0. Under the pooled decision_only message with the same private signal, p=0.5 gives v*A_psi(0.5)-c=0.50-0.56=-0.06, so non-appeal is strict.
active_margin_witness.status_basis: The disclosure_partition_delta changes the posterior from the pooled p=0.5 to the middle-cell p=0.8. Because rho_1>rho_0 and psi(1)>psi(0), A_psi(p) is strictly increasing, so the source raises the appeal payoff difference by 0.18. The strict inequalities persist on a nonempty open neighborhood of these primitives.
active_margin_witness.kill_condition: This link is inactive if score refinement never changes A_psi(p) across a positive-probability cell, or if no positive cost ratio lies strictly between the pooled and disclosed-cell acceptance probabilities.

consequence_binding.focal_consequence: The cell contributes one appeal and one positive per-appeal processing burden, and its classification consequence is realized through the fixed fresh-review rule.
consequence_binding.alternative_consequence: The same applicant remains rejected, contributes no appeal and no processing burden, and cannot generate a review-induced acceptance.
consequence_binding.feasibility_basis: The PrimitiveGraph edge appeal_choice_to_outcomes directly maps the binary decision into distinct outcome-vector contributions at this positive-probability state; the alternative contribution is zero.

causal_chain.cause: The appeal set and its qualification composition change.
causal_chain.endogenous_response: Only selected appellants receive fresh review and consume the fixed per-appeal processing burden.
causal_chain.consequence: Appeal load, appellant composition, corrected qualified rejections, and review-induced unqualified acceptances move as a vector, with no general aggregate sign imposed.

minimal_example.title: A middle-score cell enters appeal under disclosure
minimal_example.setup: For one positive-probability private-signal realization x, take p_low,x=0.2, p_pool,x=0.5, and p_middle,x=0.8. Let rho_0=0.2, rho_1=0.8, psi(0)=0, psi(1)=1, v=1, and c=0.56. Then A(p)=0.2+0.6p gives 0.32, 0.50, and 0.68 respectively.
minimal_example.moving_primitive: Only the rejected applicant's information partition moves from the pooled low-or-middle message to the realized low or middle score.
held_fixed: The prior and joint score, private-signal, and review-signal laws
The value v, appeal cost c, tie rule, and per-appeal burden
endogenous_responses: The middle-x cell appeals under score_disclosure because 0.68-0.56>0
The pooled-x cell does not appeal under decision_only because 0.50-0.56<0
The selected appeal pool and its outcome-vector contribution change
minimal_example.predicted_pattern: The middle-x cell enters appeal under score_disclosure while the pooled x cell does not; strict inequalities make this behavior robust on an open neighborhood. No sign is implied for the aggregate classification vector.
minimal_example.economic_intuition: A fixed review rule does not make disclosure neutral because applicants self-select into that rule after updating on the disclosed rejected score.
minimal_example.limitation: These illustrative posteriors demonstrate a feasible active margin but do not prove the full characterization or rank all outcome components.
cannot_establish: A universal increase or decrease in appeal volume
Formal validity of a general theorem

economist_memo.headline: Rejected-score disclosure changes selection into review
economist_memo.opening_question: When does telling a rejected applicant whether the score was low or middle change who appeals, even when review policy is fixed?
economist_memo.benchmark_message: Under decision_only, low and middle rejections are pooled before the same private signal and posterior-threshold appeal choice.
economist_memo.tension_message: Conditional review is unchanged, but the pool reaching review need not be.
economist_memo.mechanism_message: Disclosure changes the posterior, the posterior changes the binary appeal payoff, and threshold crossings change the volume and composition of appeals.
economist_memo.result_preview: Open cost intervals between pooled and score-specific acceptance probabilities generate cell entry or exit and map into a parameterized classification-processing outcome locus.
economist_memo.contribution_message: The potential contribution is a bounded characterization separating appeal quantity, composition, correction, erroneous acceptance, and processing consequences; no distinctive mechanism, welfare, or novelty claim is made at this gate.
economist_memo.scope_condition: The result is conditional on the static Bayesian scope, conditional independence baseline, score-blind strictly review-monotone final rule, and absence of capacity, congestion, rationing, or endogenous review.
economist_memo.reader_takeaway: Information disclosure can matter through endogenous selection into a fixed review technology, and its consequences should be reported as a vector rather than collapsed into an unsupported scalar ranking.

held_fixed.label: Appeal value, cost, and tie rule

benchmark_assessments.channel_summary: Relative to decision_only, score disclosure refines the posterior; the applicant reoptimizes the appeal decision; threshold crossings reweight the cases exposed to fixed review and processing.

aggregate_invariance.basis: The initial and final policy rules are pointwise fixed, but the distribution of types selecting into appeal changes with the information partition.
aggregate_invariance.implication_for_attribution: Attribution is to selection induced by the information refinement, not to an invariant aggregate or a change in review policy.

selection_assurance.selection_rule: Each rejected applicant uses the deterministic binary threshold: appeal iff v*A_psi(p)-c>=0, with ties assigned to appeal.
selection_assurance.basis: There is one individual Bayesian choice and no equilibrium correspondence or discretionary selector in the maintained frame.
selection_assurance.implication_for_attribution: The channel does not rely on choosing among multiple equilibria; all composition changes follow from the stated threshold rule.

benchmark_assessments.attribution_basis: The sole changed primitive is the rejected-score information partition; the exact active path and same-state payoff witness connect it to the appeal choice and outcome contribution while every rule and payoff is fixed.

distinctive_mechanism.basis: Decision_only and score_disclosure share the same appeal-selection mechanism. The comparison changes the information entering that mechanism, so no mechanism absent from the contrast is claimed.

payload.action_rationale: The exact decision_only benchmark is covered once; the only changed primitive has a connected active posterior-to-appeal payoff witness and a bound outcome consequence; aggregate reweighting is disclosed rather than held fixed; the minimal example supplies a nonempty open margin; and the scope now uses an outcome-vector comparison/locus without capacity or optimization. This is only an audit proposal for human G1 consideration, not a G1 decision.

entity.title: Replacement G1 dossier after outcome-locus framing audit
entity.summary: An unconfirmed G1 dossier that appends the fresh positive framing audit without mutating the pre-audit dossier.

payload.rationale: The fresh audit proposes that the exact question, decision_only benchmark, and Scheme-B decomposition are ready for the researcher to consider at G1. The audit supplies a connected active-margin witness and retains all no-sign, no-welfare, no-capacity, and outcome-locus boundaries. No human G1 decision is recorded or implied.

requirements.description: The question identifies the rejected-score disclosure perturbation, the appeal-selection object, and the separate classification and processing outcomes without imposing a directional or welfare claim.
The benchmark set contains exactly decision_only, and score_disclosure changes only the rejected applicant's information partition while every other maintained primitive is fixed.

The potential importance is a transparent separation of appeal volume, appellant composition, corrected qualified rejections, erroneous final acceptances, and processing burden; this is a bounded contribution radius rather than a claim about transparency, welfare, or policy generally.

The researcher selected the score-blind class phi(s,r)=psi(r), psi(1)>psi(0), with phi(s,r)=r as the deterministic baseline; PrimitiveGraph version 5 preserves its implied acceptance probability and appeal payoff.

Park or kill the framing if no admissible nonempty open region changes a positive-probability appeal cell, or if all results remain mechanical threshold enumeration with no interpretable posterior ordering, selection decomposition, or sufficient statistic.

The current question and PrimitiveGraph describe a parameterized classification-processing outcome-vector comparison/locus, not a strict feasible-set or optimization frontier. A positive per-appeal burden does not add capacity, rationing, congestion, endogenous review, or welfare aggregation; any such mechanism would require a later human framing decision.
No human G1 decision is recorded in this dossier; a human must confirm the core question, exact benchmark scope, and any final-review primitive before they become stable dependencies.

The fresh framing-economics audit binds an active posterior-threshold appeal margin to the institutional outcome vector, discloses endogenous composition, and preserves the outcome-locus and no-capacity boundaries.

outcome.rationale: The fresh V8 audit binds the exact outcome-locus frame to one active posterior-threshold appeal margin and its institutional outcome-vector consequence, preserves aggregate endogeneity and all no-capacity/no-optimization boundaries, and only proposes human G1 consideration.
